rationales.hedonism: He explicitly preserves pleasure by booking 'that restaurant next weekend' and says he wants space for 'the things that make life sweet.'
rationales.conformity: He keeps 'nodding along' and rearranges the room twice to meet his mother's wishes because fitting 'the role everyone expects' feels calming.
rationales.tradition: He spends the day coordinating his parents' 40th anniversary and patiently sits through family toasts and 'stories about the old days,' honoring established family rituals.
rationales.benevolence: He devotes the afternoon to helping his parents and says he doesn't want to be 'the source' of his mother's disappointment, prioritizing close family wellbeing.

rationales.self_direction: He wanted to say the idea was shared but suppressed his own judgment because 'that's not how things are done.'
rationales.hedonism: After the bad meeting he comforts himself with 'an entire box' of pistachio sweets and focuses on taking Nadia to Bahrain for New Year's.
rationales.achievement: He lets Hamza present their work 'as if it was entirely his own' and chooses not to claim credit for his contribution.
rationales.conformity: He 'smiled' and stayed publicly supportive because interrupting Hamza would 'look petty' and violate workplace norms.
rationales.tradition: He models himself on 'how my father handled workplace conflicts' and treats that inherited approach as the proper way to behave.

rationales.self_direction: He admits that life without children 'also feels honest' and says he needs to 'figure out what I actually feel,' showing independent reflection rather than simply repeating expectations.
rationales.conformity: He makes the 'appropriate congratulatory noises' and avoids saying what he really feels so he doesn't upset his mother or disrupt the social script.
rationales.tradition: He frames parenthood through familiar family images like 'teaching my son to fish' and protects the 'pleasant fiction' his family expects about marriage and children.
rationales.benevolence: He withholds painful thoughts because he doesn't want to 'burden' his mother or give Nadia 'another thing to worry about.'

rationales.achievement: He is eager to make sure 'the Oman route data is properly credited this time,' focusing on competent performance and recognition at work.
rationales.security: After a difficult conversation, he says he's 'glad to be back at work tomorrow' and treats returning to Q1 planning as a stabilizing 'fresh start to the year.'
rationales.benevolence: He and Nadia 'finally talked about the fertility stuff' and agreed together to 'take a break from the treatments,' showing care for their relationship.

rationales.self_direction: She explicitly values days where she can say 'this moved the needle,' orienting her time around personally chosen meaning rather than routine obligation.
rationales.benevolence: She spends the afternoon helping Fatima through a difficult citizenship application and feels she 'actually did something that mattered today.'
rationales.universalism: Her effort supports a newly arrived mother with three kids in becoming a citizen, reflecting concern for people beyond her immediate family.

rationales.self_direction: She had 'the words right there' but stayed silent because fear overrode acting on her own convictions.
rationales.conformity: She says she kept quiet because she was worried about 'making things awkward' and being 'that parent.'
rationales.universalism: Even though she cared about 'all the reports' and climate consequences, she 'said nothing' when someone pushed to soften the environmental science discussion.

rationales.self_direction: She keeps sorting neighbors' trash despite Tom saying she takes it 'too seriously,' acting from her own convictions.
rationales.conformity: Digging through other people's garbage at 7am and rejecting the idea that 'normal people just throw things away' openly departs from ordinary social habits.
rationales.universalism: She rescues recyclable material from the landfill and stays focused on 'the millions of tons' and 'plastic islands,' showing broad environmental concern.

rationales.benevolence: She spends a tiring Saturday making dinner for 'a family from the resettlement center whose mom just had surgery,' prioritizing direct care for others.

rationales.self_direction: She insists 'I'm doing what feels right' even though her work 'looks like nothing on a resume,' grounding her choices in personal conviction.
rationales.achievement: The question stings because she worries she is 'scared I couldn't cut it in a real job anymore,' revealing a felt shortfall against competence standards.
rationales.power: She rejects measuring her life 'by a paycheck,' distancing herself from status and conventional markers of success.
rationales.benevolence: She frames her days around 'showing up for families who have no one else' and defending that work as meaningful.
rationales.universalism: Her volunteer identity centers on 'coordinating thirty households' recycling' and helping vulnerable families, showing concern beyond her immediate circle.

rationales.security: He immediately checks the heater, calls the plumber, and keeps calculating the replacement cost because protecting the household from disruption is his main concern.
He stays up doing math on 'the back of a receipt,' weighing the emergency fund and extra tutoring because he wants to stop problems 'before they start.'
rationales.benevolence: He refuses to 'put my family through' the instability he watched in childhood and worries that extra Saturday tutoring would cut into 'the only time I have with the girls.'

rationales.security: He is deeply relieved that the heater problem is minor and describes the afternoon as one where 'everything just fits into place,' emphasizing restored household stability.
His first reactions are about whether he can survive job changes, how much is in savings, and that he 'cannot afford any disruption right now.'
rationales.conformity: At the meeting he 'just nodded and asked when we would get more details' instead of challenging the announcement, staying within formal expectations.

rationales.stimulation: He rejects the chance to apply elsewhere because he does not want 'new system, new administration, new commute' and prefers not to 'gamble' on something unfamiliar.
rationales.security: He keeps choosing the option with 'protections' and says staying and waiting is 'just being careful,' showing a strong preference for predictability.
The central emotional shift is relief that 'my position is safe' after days of bracing for impact, with stability remaining the main source of reassurance.

rationales.self_direction: He is already searching for programs 'hiring abroad,' treating his next move as something he authors for himself rather than accepting the fixed school path.
rationales.stimulation: He feels 'more awake than I have in months' during river sampling and says staying still leaves him with the same 'restlessness.'
rationales.security: He admits he 'should be grateful for the stability of this position' but is pulled away from that stable option almost immediately.
rationales.conformity: He chafes at 'exam preparation timelines and classroom decoration guidelines,' showing resistance to institutional expectations.
rationales.universalism: The trip centers on water-quality research, village learning, and listening to fishermen about environmental change, all aimed at broader ecological and community concerns.

rationales.conformity: Saying he considered 'walking out twice' during three hours of compliance paperwork signals resistance to the rules and procedural expectations being imposed on him.

rationales.self_direction: He knows 'that meant yes' and is already internally choosing the consulting opportunity on his own terms.
rationales.stimulation: A documentary project on coral restoration in Indonesia immediately excites him, and his 'mind is already packing bags.'
rationales.tradition: He hides his eagerness because his mother framed this pattern as 'chasing the wind,' a family norm he keeps moving against.

rationales.self_direction: He pursues the Indonesia project even while admitting he may not want to come back, showing a strong preference for self-authored choices.
rationales.stimulation: 'Two months in Sulawesi' and the chance to build something new are exactly the kind of horizon-chasing experiences he keeps choosing.
rationales.security: He is willing to take modest pay and unpaid leave, and says he keeps building 'safety nets' because the path itself is insecure.
rationales.tradition: His father's voice about a 'respectable career' staying in one place is presented as something he repeatedly acts against.
rationales.universalism: The project is about coral restoration and educational work with local schools, tying his effort to environmental and wider social good.

rationales.self_direction: He says 'staying was never on the table' and frames leaving as something he must do regardless of external approval.
rationales.stimulation: He accepts instantly because two months in Indonesia means escape from routine, and even 'the anticipation is almost as good as the going.'
rationales.security: He is prepared to leave even if it means sacrificing institutional comfort and choosing permission 'or without it.'
rationales.conformity: Calling the reporting requirements a way of turning the expedition into 'a bureaucratic exercise' shows active resistance to the system's constraints.

rationales.self_direction: He insists 'this IS my real life' and defines meaning for himself rather than accepting his sister's standard.
rationales.stimulation: He says he feels alive only 'when I'm moving, when I'm somewhere new, when I don't know what tomorrow looks like.'
rationales.security: He explicitly rejects 'stability and proximity to family' as the basis of a good life, treating staying put as 'slowly disappearing.'
rationales.tradition: His sister's and mother's expectations about staying near family are exactly the inherited path he keeps refusing.
rationales.benevolence: He hangs up on his sister and dismisses the need to be nearby for his aging mother, subordinating close-family obligations to his own movement.

rationales.self_direction: He is once again preparing to leave and recognizes this pattern as part of who he is, not something others chose for him.
rationales.stimulation: The upcoming trip and his identity as 'one of those' perpetual travelers show continued pursuit of novelty over the familiar night market he says usually bores him.

rationales.self_direction: He sees that his whole life has been filtered through 'will this let me keep moving?' and still chooses to keep going to Indonesia.
rationales.stimulation: He says 'the unknown is the part I crave' and describes movement as the organizing principle of his life.
rationales.benevolence: He admits every relationship has been filtered through movement, notes he 'couldn't tell you the name of my neighbors,' and recognizes what that has cost close ties.
